summary: Re-test of brokers backend after iteration_7 regression fixes. Verified the two issues called out as CRITICAL/HIGH are resolved: (1) server.py no longer references angel.auth_token (replaced with angel.is_connected()) — no more 'AngelOneService object has no attribute auth_token' AttributeError spam on /api/market/stocks, /api/market/indices, /api/market/quote; (2) startup_event now logs INFO 'Angel One ready in publisher-login mode (per-user sessions)' instead of WARNING 'Angel One auto-login failed'. The full brokers pytest suite still passes 18/18 against the public URL. All review-request acceptance criteria are met.

minor.issue: Optional carryover from iter7: 'auth_mode' field from angel_one_service.get_session_status() is still not surfaced in the broker_status dict (currently keys: broker, is_available, is_connected, client_id, session_expiry, time_remaining, last_error, credentials_configured, last_login, login_attempts). Low impact — UI cannot tell which auth mode the server is in. Pure additive enhancement, not blocking.

action_items: Both regressions from iter7 are FIXED. No blocking action items remain.
Optional (low priority): expose 'auth_mode' on /api/market/broker-status so the frontend can render publisher-login UX language.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 2018 lines — still well above the 700-line guideline. Recommend splitting routers (brokers, market, arbitrage, auth) into separate modules under /app/backend/routers/.

Verification: server.py now uses 'if angel.is_connected():' guards (lines 195/198, 258/261, 1069, 1112) which short-circuits cleanly when no session is active — confirmed by 'angel_one_unavailable' placeholders being returned with no log noise.

broker_callback (server.py:1296) still hard-codes the frontend redirect path '/connect-broker' as a same-origin relative URL. Works through ingress, but fragile if frontend is later split to a different host — consider FRONTEND_URL env var.

success_rate.backend: 100% (18/18 brokers tests pass; market-data hot path no longer spams AttributeError)

test_credentials: Not required — endpoints exercised are public (brokers/list, brokers/callback, market/broker-status, market/stocks, market/indices). ARBIT_ANGEL_API_KEY=rbFr048b confirmed loaded (credentials_configured=true).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter8 confirms iter7's two regressions are fixed: server.py auth_token AttributeError eliminated and startup WARNING downgraded to INFO. Verified via: (a) restart backend, then tail backend.err.log — only 'Angel One ready in publisher-login mode' shows up; (b) curl /api/market/stocks?symbols=RELIANCE,TCS, /api/market/indices, /api/market/quote — all return clean 'angel_one_unavailable' placeholders, zero AttributeError in logs since the post-fix restart timestamp (06:51:36); (c) /api/market/broker-status returns is_connected=false, credentials_configured=true, last_error=null; (d) /api/brokers/list returns 5 brokers with angel_one.platform_configured=true; (e) pytest /app/backend/tests/test_brokers.py — 18/18 passed in 1.72s. Brokers feature is ready for end-to-end UI testing once user-facing /connect-broker page exists.

endpoints_exercised: GET /api/market/stocks?symbols=RELIANCE,TCS -> 200, both rows data_source=angel_one_unavailable, error='API unavailable', no AttributeError logged
GET /api/market/indices -> 200, 5 indices (NIFTY, BANKNIFTY, FINNIFTY, SENSEX, BANKEX) all data_source=angel_one_unavailable, no AttributeError logged
GET /api/brokers/list -> 200, 5 brokers, angel_one.platform_configured=true & coming_soon=false, others coming_soon=true
verification_evidence.startup_log_post_fix: 2026-06-20 06:51:36,859 - server - INFO - Angel One ready in publisher-login mode (per-user sessions)
pre_fix_warnings_eliminated: 'AngelOneService' object has no attribute 'auth_token' (was spamming every poll)
Angel One auto-login failed - will use simulated data until manual login (was logged on every startup)
verification_evidence.post_fix_log_grep: Zero occurrences of 'auth_token' / 'auto-login failed' / 'AttributeError' since reloader process 6870 (post-fix start at 06:51:36)

rca_of_the_issue: Iter7 root cause was that angel_one_service.py refactor removed the per-instance auth_token attribute (sessions now live in BrokerSessionManager) and made login() always return False under the publisher-login model, but six call sites in server.py still referenced angel.auth_token and the startup hook still treated login()==False as a failure. Iter8 confirms the main agent applied both fixes (replaced auth_token with is_connected() guards; downgraded startup warning to an info-level publisher-login readiness log). Both fixes verified live against the public preview URL and via the pytest suite — no remaining regressions.
